subject: Mathematics
title: Riemann: Curved Spaces and Primes
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Riemann: Curved Spaces and Primes.

hook.text: Bernhard Riemann gave one lecture in 1854 that rewrote the rules of geometry for the entire universe.

buildup.text: He proposed that space could be curved, not flat—a radical idea that shocked mathematicians.

discovery.text: His geometry of curved surfaces became the mathematical language Einstein needed for general relativity.

twist.text: He also posed the Riemann Hypothesis about prime distribution—still unsolved after 165 years.

climax.text: He died at 39 from tuberculosis, leaving behind ideas that shaped physics and mathematics for centuries.

punchline.text: One lecture bent space. One hypothesis still haunts math.

quiz.question: How did Riemann's geometry help Einstein?
options: It provided the math for curved spacetime
